nativeRange.habitat: Trade hybrid — no wild population. Parental Aulonocara stock originates from sandy and rocky benthic zones of Lake Malawi, Africa, at depths of 3–25 m.
nativeRange.biotope: Lake Malawi (ancestral — hybrid strain has no wild biotope)

decorPreferences: sandy substrate (fine play sand or pool filter sand, minimum 5 cm depth)
stacked rock caves and overhangs for territory and hiding
minimal or no live plants (alkaline water and digging damage plants)

diet.notes: Aulonocara peacocks are benthic carnivores that use lateral-line electroreception to detect invertebrates buried in substrate. Feed high-quality cichlid pellets or granules, supplemented with frozen/live foods such as brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, bloodworms, and cyclops. Avoid excessive feeding of bloodworms (linked to Malawi bloat). Spirulina-enhanced foods are acceptable as a small portion of the diet. Do not feed beef heart or other mammalian proteins; high-protein animal fats are associated with internal bacterial infections (Malawi bloat) in Rift Lake cichlids.

goodWith: other Aulonocara peacock species and hybrids (mixed peacock tank)
Haplochromine (Hap) cichlids of similar temperament and size
Synodontis multipunctatus and other Rift Lake catfish
Bristlenose plecos (Ancistrus sp.) as algae control
avoidWith: Mbuna (rock-dwelling cichlids — persistent aggression toward less assertive peacocks)
aggressive Haplochromines that may bully peacocks (e.g., Nimbochromis, large Copadichromis)
small fish (will be eaten or harassed)
other Aulonocara male OBs (intense conspecific male aggression; keep one male or maintain species-ratio carefully)

summary: The OB Peacock is a captive-bred trade hybrid between Aulonocara peacocks and Mbuna cichlids, expressing the X-linked orange-blotch (OB) gene. Most OB-expressing specimens sold are female; true OB males are rare. Kept in Malawi hard-alkaline water with rocky décor and open sand.
careNotes: The OB Peacock is not a valid species but a captive trade hybrid produced by crossing Aulonocara peacocks (most commonly A. stuartgranti, A. nyassae, or related Peacock species) with Mbuna cichlids (rock-dwelling Lake Malawi cichlids, typically Metriaclima or Pseudotropheus lineages) to introduce the X-linked orange-blotch (OB) color gene. The resulting fish display irregular orange-and-black marbled patterning often compared to calico cats, hence the alternative name "Marmalade Peacock." Because the OB locus is X-linked, the gene is far more commonly expressed in females (XX); males (XY) require the gene on both sex chromosomes to express it visually, making true male OB Peacocks uncommon. Many fish marketed as "OB Peacock males" in the hobby are actually females or are mislabeled. Genuine OB males typically show brilliant electric-blue or gold base coloration overlaid with orange blotch. Husbandry follows Aulonocara standards: maintain high pH (7.8–8.6), hard alkaline water (GH 10–20, KH 8–18), and temperature 24–28 °C. A fine sandy substrate of at least 5 cm is important; Aulonocara forage by hovering over sand and using lateral-line electroreception to detect invertebrate movement below the surface. Provide stacked rock architecture for territory demarcation and visual barriers to reduce aggression. A minimum 190 L (50 gal US) tank with 120 cm footprint is recommended for a small group. Keep one male with multiple females or in a community Malawi Peacock/Hap tank; do not mix with Mbuna, which will outcompete and harass peacocks. Note merge candidate: fw-fish-206 ob-peacock-cichlid-aulonocara (Aulonocara stuartgranti) describes the same trade strain; both entries should be reviewed for consolidation.
breedingNotes: Maternal mouthbrooder. Spawning follows typical Aulonocara lek behavior: the male clears a flat sandy spawning site, displays to females, and induces the female to lay eggs which are immediately taken into the mouth. The female incubates 20–40 eggs orally for approximately 21–28 days at 26 °C, refusing food throughout. Fry are released as free-swimming juveniles large enough to accept crushed cichlid fry food and baby brine shrimp nauplii. Breeding in captivity is readily achieved with a 1:3 or 1:4 male-to-female ratio; remove the holding female to a separate tank to prevent harassment and allow her to release fry safely. Females will 'spit' prematurely under stress. OB-expressing female offspring will carry the gene; roughly half of male offspring from an OB female will carry an OB allele on one X chromosome but will not visually express it unless also carrying the gene paternally.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op — African Cichlid Care Guide
primary.notes: Primary trade reference for Aulonocara peacock cichlid husbandry parameters, tank sizing, diet, and community compatibility. OB Peacock hybrid notes drawn from general peacock cichlid guidance, corroborated with species-specific sources below.

additional.name: SeriouslyFish — Aulonocara stuartgranti
additional.notes: Wild parental taxon reference. Provided water parameters (pH 7.5–8.5, temp 24–26 °C, hardness 10–20 °dH), adult size (males to 15 cm), sandy-substrate and depth data, diet (benthic invertebrates via lateral-line detection), and maternal mouthbrooding details. OB hybrid is derived from this and related Aulonocara; husbandry parameters treated as directly applicable. Merge candidate: this species (A. stuartgranti, catalogued as fw-fish-206) covers the same trade product.

additional.name: FishBase — Aulonocara stuartgranti
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Cichlidae, Cichliformes), adult size (males to 15 cm TL), native range (Lake Malawi, Malawi/Tanzania/Mozambique), temperature (22–26 °C), pH range, depth range (3–25 m benthic zones), diet classification (invertivore/carnivore). Used as parental-species baseline for hybrid parameters.

additional.name: Practical Fishkeeping — OB Peacock cichlid
additional.notes: Hobbyist source confirming OB trade strain origin as Mbuna × Aulonocara hybrid, X-linked OB gene sex bias (females predominantly express), Marmalade Peacock trade name, and typical care in Malawi hard-water setups. Noted typical group sizing, male aggression management, and compatibility guidance with Hap cichlids.

additional.name: TFH Magazine — Peacock Cichlids of Lake Malawi
additional.notes: Background on Aulonocara genus ecology, lateral-line foraging behavior over sandy substrate, lek spawning behavior, and mouthbrooding reproductive strategy. Contextual reference for OB hybrid behavioral traits inherited from Aulonocara lineage.

fish.breedingNotes: Maternal mouthbrooder; 21–28 day oral incubation, 20–40 fry per brood. Separate holding female to prevent stress-spit. 1:3–4 male-to-female ratio recommended. Fry accept baby brine shrimp and crushed fry food on release.
